human_actions_pending.context: cv2 imshow needs full session context (D-bus, XDG_SESSION_*) which the Bash tool's subshell doesn't reliably provide. Launching from a real terminal makes the Merged Detection window appear normally.

decisions.decision: wrist_roll +/- toggle = camera-angle for marker visibility (not drop-orientation or kinematics)
decisions.rationale: User clarification: + is the optimal cup-markers-in-FOV pose; - is the alternate when + is occluded. Default +.
Robust to occlusion. If only 2 of 3 markers detected, update those 2, keep previously-cached value for the third. User-chosen via AskUserQuestion.

decisions.decision: Real Test tab in vla_SO-ARM101 control_gui.py — testing in isolation, refactor later
decisions.rationale: User suggested approach: build all real-mode buttons in one new tab, validate end-to-end, then move blocks into respective sections of existing UI. Lower risk than weaving into existing tabs immediately.

decisions.decision: _objects_poses_real (YOLOE) is sole source for lego positions in real mode — even if it deviates from sim ground truth
decisions.rationale: User correction during session: real mode mocks the real workflow, sim ground truth is irrelevant. The whole point is to validate real-pipeline accuracy. Don't fall back to /objects_poses_sim.

decisions.decision: Don't modify _cmd_drop_sweep — variant lives in new Run Real Pick & Drop handler
decisions.rationale: Keep sim mode untouched. New flow has its own variant that substitutes _cached_cup_poses for the live /drop_poses topic lookup.

decisions.decision: Camera intrinsics aligned at 1280x720 (matches active-viewport publisher emit + real RealSense)
decisions.rationale: Originally 640x480 in YAML, 1280x720 publisher emit. Mismatch caused 2x scale errors in PnP. YAML now bumped to 1280x720 — sim and real share same intrinsics.
Origin's hardcoded 0.032 was for JETANK markers. SO-ARM101 cup markers are 25mm per CUP_ARUCO_CONFIG in soarm101-dt extension.py:391-401. Hardcoded fix; ideally should be config-driven per-robot.

decisions.decision: Cup marker offset in aruco_config.json: Y=-0.005, Z=-0.033 (computed from sim ground truth)
decisions.rationale: User's prior uncommitted Y=+0.083 was geometrically invalid (would land below floor). Recomputed by reading marker mesh bbox + cup body-center bbox from sim — produces 5mm up, 33mm into cup body (marker is inset 6mm into wall, not flush).

decisions.decision: Sim /drop_poses frame_id changed from World → base via /World/base passthrough Xform prim
decisions.rationale: Aligns with /ee_pose convention and real localizer's frame_id='base'. Small addition to _cmd_publish_drop_poses (extension.py:5774-5786).

decisions.decision: _ViewportCameraPublisher.start() now picks main 'Viewport' window explicitly, not get_active_viewport()
decisions.rationale: get_active_viewport() returned 'Viewport 2' (programmatic, hidden) while UI showed 'Viewport' (Persp). Subscription bound to wrong viewport caused topic to publish Persp content despite metadata claiming wrist. Fix iterates get_viewport_window_instances().

next_action: Resume task #11 (Real Test tab: add tab + state vars). Open /home/aaugus11/Projects/Exploring-VLAs/vla_SO-ARM101/src/so_arm101_control/so_arm101_control/control_gui.py. Find the existing tab-build pattern (_build_grasp_tab is one example, look for the ttk.Notebook .add() calls) and add a new _build_real_test_tab method following the same conventions. Add instance vars _drop_wrist_roll_sign=+1 and _cached_cup_poses={} in __init__. Tab body: empty placeholder with a label 'Real Test (under construction)' for now. After verifying tab shows in GUI, proceed to tasks 12 (Open Gripper), 14 (Grasp Home), 13 (Drop Point Green) — easy wires to existing services.
context_notes: User's mental model: real-mode mocks real-world workflow. Cameras can't see everything always — must scan-then-cache. Cup poses are detected ONCE per cycle at scan pose (drop_point on green cup, where wrist camera sees all 3 markers), pushed into MoveIt as collision objects, then never refreshed. Lego poses refresh every cycle from /objects_poses_real (YOLOE). Both detection sources are 'authoritative' for real mode regardless of how they compare with sim ground truth. Build all this in a fresh Real Test tab in vla_SO-ARM101 control_gui.py, validate end-to-end, then refactor blocks into existing UI sections. Order: scaffold → simple wires (Open Gripper, Grasp Home, Drop Point Green) → wrist_roll +/- toggle → Refresh Cups Pose with MoveIt push → Run Real Pick & Drop loop.

anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: get_active_viewport() reported wrist_camera but actual rendered frames came from a different viewport (Persp). Spent significant time analyzing camera-frame math when the real bug was that the topic was publishing the wrong camera entirely.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: When pose math seems wildly wrong, always verify the IMAGE STREAM matches expectation before deeper analysis. Capture and visually inspect the raw topic content.

anti_patterns_discovered.pattern: Hardcoded values in 'verified' upstream code masquerading as bugs in our setup
anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: MARKER_SIZE = 0.032 was hardcoded for JETANK in origin's localizer. Looked correct (origin verified) but was wrong for SO-ARM101's 25mm markers. Origin's so_arm101 path was half-finished (config block existed, hardcodes weren't parameterized).
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: When using upstream code 'verified for X' on a different robot Y, audit hardcoded constants for X-specific values. Don't assume 'verified' means 'parameterized for all robots'.

anti_patterns_discovered.pattern: Multiple parallel clones of the same repo with divergent uncommitted edits
anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: Two clones of aruco_camera_localizer (Projects/RoboSort vs Desktop/ros2_ws/src). Bashrc sourced Desktop's install. My edits to Projects/RoboSort were ignored at runtime because Desktop's PYTHONPATH took priority. Discovered only after env -i diagnostic.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: When multiple clones exist, identify which one is on the runtime PYTHONPATH BEFORE editing. Run `python3 -c 'import pkg; print(pkg.__file__)'` to confirm. Consolidate to one clone when possible.
